team_members.name: Pavel Durov
team_members.role: Original Founder (TON concept / Telegram); not active TON Foundation lead
team_members.linkedin_url: NOT_AVAILABLE_FREE_TIER — Durov has no public LinkedIn; identity confirmed via Telegram blog and press
team_members.notes: Durov and brother Nikolai created the original TON (Telegram Open Network) in 2018. After SEC enforcement action, Telegram abandoned the project in 2020. The open-source code was picked up by the community and rebranded as 'The Open Network'. Durov is NOT a formal officer of the TON Foundation. Arrested in France Aug 2024 on unrelated Telegram moderation charges.

team_members.name: Nikolai Durov
team_members.role: Original Protocol Architect (TON whitepaper author); not active in TON Foundation
team_members.notes: Authored the original TON whitepapers (2017-2018). Considered the technical genius behind the architecture. No current formal role in TON Foundation.

team_members.name: Steve Yun
team_members.linkedin_url: https://www.linkedin.com/in/steveyun/ — profile exists but details sparse; confirmed via TON Foundation blog
team_members.notes: Appointed as TON Foundation President ~2022. Background in Korean tech/blockchain ecosystem. Primary public face of the Foundation.

team_members.name: Anatoly Makosov
team_members.notes: One of the key open-source contributors who revived the project post-Telegram abandonment. Active on GitHub (ton-blockchain org).

team_members.name: Kirill Emelyanenko
team_members.notes: Listed as core contributor on TON GitHub. Limited public profile.

investors.notes: Telegram raised ~$1.7B in two private placement rounds (2018) from accredited investors for GRAM tokens. SEC halted distribution. Telegram refunded ~$1.2B and paid $18.5M SEC penalty. Original GRAM investors did NOT receive TON tokens in the community relaunch.
Initial TON supply distributed via Proof-of-Work mining (givers). No VC round for the community chain. Tokens earned by miners are fully liquid.

Foundation holds a portion of supply for ecosystem grants and development. Exact wallet addresses partially trackable on-chain via Tonscan.org. Vesting/unlock schedule not fully disclosed in public docs — PARTIAL data.
Pantera has publicly stated TON exposure via ecosystem investments; specific round terms NOT disclosed on free-tier Crunchbase or public filings.
Reported ecosystem/strategic investor. Terms not publicly available.
Listed as ecosystem partner/investor in TON Foundation announcements. Financial terms undisclosed.

legal_events.description: SEC obtained emergency restraining order against Telegram Group Inc. and TON Issuer Inc., alleging unregistered securities offering of GRAM tokens (~$1.7B raise). Case: SEC v. Telegram Group Inc., 1:19-cv-09439.
Telegram settled with SEC: agreed to return ~$1.2B to investors, pay $18.5M civil penalty, and provide 45-day notice before any future digital asset sales. Telegram formally abandoned TON project.

Community developers (NewTON / Free TON) forked and relaunched the open-source TON codebase independently of Telegram, rebranding eventually as 'The Open Network'. This legally separated the chain from Telegram's SEC issues.

Pavel Durov arrested at Paris Le Bourget airport by French authorities. Charges related to alleged facilitation of criminal activity on Telegram (not TON-specific). Released on €5M bail. Ongoing investigation. Creates reputational/association risk for TON given Telegram integration.

SEC EDGAR search for 'TON Foundation' and 'The Open Network' returns NO current filings. The community chain has not registered any securities with the SEC. GRAM settlement applies to Telegram, not the community TON chain. Regulatory status of TON token under US law remains unresolved/untested.

founder_credibility_summary: The original founders (Pavel and Nikolai Durov) are highly credible technologists with proven track records (VK, Telegram) but are legally and formally disconnected from the current TON Foundation following the 2020 SEC settlement. The current TON Foundation leadership (Steve Yun et al.) has limited public track record compared to the Durov brand, and the project's credibility is heavily dependent on Telegram's 950M+ user distribution — a dependency that introduces concentration risk, especially given Durov's August 2024 French legal troubles.
vc_overhang_summary: Unlike most L1s, TON's community chain was NOT launched via a traditional VC-funded token sale, meaning there is no large cliff-vesting VC overhang in the conventional sense. However, the TON Foundation treasury and undisclosed strategic investor allocations (Pantera, Mirana, KuCoin Ventures) represent opaque supply-side risk; unlock schedules for these parties are NOT publicly available on free-tier sources, making precise overhang quantification impossible.
alignment_summary: Retail and insider incentives are PARTIALLY aligned but with notable caveats. The PoW mining launch gave early community participants fair access, which is positive. However, the Foundation treasury's undisclosed vesting, the heavy dependency on Telegram's commercial decisions (mini-apps, TON Space wallet), and the lack of transparent governance mean insiders (Foundation, Telegram) retain significant unilateral influence over ecosystem direction and token utility. Durov's legal situation adds an exogenous misalignment risk: adverse Telegram outcomes could suppress TON adoption regardless of protocol fundamentals.
sources: https://github.com/ton-blockchain — TON core GitHub org (contributor data)
https://tonscan.org — On-chain explorer for Foundation wallet tracking
